# circt4coral v0.1.0

ðŸŽ‰ **First public release of the standalone MLIRâ†’CoralNPU compiler**

The `circt4coral` binary is a 211MB statically-linked executable that lowers TOSA / CoralNPU MLIR to RISC-V assembly with RVV + custom CSRs.

## What's in this release

- âœ… **7/7 e2e tests pass** on spike + CoralNPU patches
- âœ… **TOSAâ†’CoralNPU pipeline** working (tosa.add demonstrated)
- âœ… **Custom CSR codegen** for KISA (0xFC0) and KSCM0-4 (0xFC4-0xFD4)
- âœ… **Standalone binary**: no LLVM/MLIR install required
- âœ… **Docker teaching image** for 5-minute setup
- âœ… **GitHub Actions CI** validating every commit

## Quick start

```bash
# Download the binary
wget https://github.com/lipeng821020589/Circt4Coral/releases/download/v0.1.0/circt4coral
chmod +x circt4coral
./circt4coral --help

# Compile your first MLIR
./circt4coral hello.mlir --emit-asm
```

## Build from source (optional)

```bash
git clone --recursive https://github.com/lipeng821020589/Circt4Coral.git
cd Circt4Coral
./bin/build-circt4coral.sh
```

## Architecture

```
TOSA MLIR
   â”‚  tosa-to-coralnpu
   â–¼
CoralNPU dialect
   â”‚  stripmine + legalize + regalloc
   â–¼
Canonical CoralNPU
   â”‚  emit-coralnpu-assembly
   â–¼
RISC-V assembly (RV32IMV + KISA/KSCM CSRs)
   â”‚  riscv64-linux-gnu-as
   â–¼
ELF binary
   â”‚  spike --isa=rv32imf_v_zvl128b_zicsr_zifencei
   â–¼
exit 0 âœ“
```
